docs.text: This is the main page for api references and usage for everything I made to the public. NOTE that some stuff uses someone's stuff, I do credit all of them. Please excuse any typo that I made. If you find any bugs on the site or at one of my module, make a new issue on Roblox-Stuff . Public modules: ¶ Color Picker Module Custom Events
docs.title: Home

docs.text: Color Picker Module Custom Events
docs.title: Public modules:

docs.text: This is the page for all API References of Color Picker Module. For usage of how to use see the usage page [Bool/Color3/BrickColor/Dictionary/Number] [Yieldable] module.promptPickColor(player, allowCancel, returnType) ¶ Important: This function will pause the Lua thread until the player has finished the prompt or canceled the prompt. Tip allowCancel and returnType is a optional argument, however the player argument is required. Prompts the player to pick a color. This will yield the script until the prompt is finished/canceled. Arguments: player - [Required] [Player/string] The player object / player name (suggested using the player object instead) allowCancel - [Optional] [Bool] If true, this will allow the player to cancel the prompt. If not boolean, it will default to true returnType - [Optional] [returnType] The type to return, you must use module.Enum.returnType . Returns: - Color3 if no returnType is specified/returnType is Color3 BrickColor if returnType is brickColor Dictionary if returnType is rgbTableDictionary Single number per RGB if returnType is perValue false if player has canceled the prompt/player doesn't exist. [returnType] module.Enum.returnType ¶ The return type to be used at module.promptPickColor() There are currently 4 options: brickColor - returnType BrickColor color3 - returnType Color3 rgbTableDictionary - returnType Dictionary table perValue - returnType number Note You should be using this with module.promptPickColor() if you want.
docs.title: API Reference

docs.text: Important: This function will pause the Lua thread until the player has finished the prompt or canceled the prompt. Tip allowCancel and returnType is a optional argument, however the player argument is required. Prompts the player to pick a color. This will yield the script until the prompt is finished/canceled. Arguments: player - [Required] [Player/string] The player object / player name (suggested using the player object instead) allowCancel - [Optional] [Bool] If true, this will allow the player to cancel the prompt. If not boolean, it will default to true returnType - [Optional] [returnType] The type to return, you must use module.Enum.returnType . Returns: - Color3 if no returnType is specified/returnType is Color3 BrickColor if returnType is brickColor Dictionary if returnType is rgbTableDictionary Single number per RGB if returnType is perValue false if player has canceled the prompt/player doesn't exist.
docs.title: [Bool/Color3/BrickColor/Dictionary/Number] [Yieldable] module.promptPickColor(player, allowCancel, returnType)

docs.text: The return type to be used at module.promptPickColor() There are currently 4 options: brickColor - returnType BrickColor color3 - returnType Color3 rgbTableDictionary - returnType Dictionary table perValue - returnType number Note You should be using this with module.promptPickColor() if you want.
docs.title: [returnType] module.Enum.returnType

docs.text: I have made this module to simplify asking users to pick a color. This module should be used on the server. Credits: boatbomber - Updated EgoMoose 's color picker to be better. EgoMoose - Initial color picker system. ItzEthanPlayz_YT - Turning it to a module. You can get it here . Pages ¶ API Reference Usage of how to use
docs.title: Color Picker Module

docs.text: API Reference Usage of how to use
docs.title: Pages

docs.text: This page shows you how to use Color Picker Module. Basic usage using player object ¶ local module = require ( PATH_TO_MODULE ) local players = game : GetService ( "Players" ) local player1 = Players : FindFirstChild ( "Player1" ) local colorResult = module . promptPickColor ( player1 , true ) print ( colorResult ) Basic usage using player name ¶ local module = require ( PATH_TO_MODULE ) local playerName = "Player1" local colorResult = module . promptPickColor ( playerName , true ) print ( colorResult ) Non-cancelable prompt ¶ local module = require ( PATH_TO_MODULE ) local players = game : GetService ( "Players" ) local player1 = Players : FindFirstChild ( "Player1" ) local colorResult = module . promptPickColor ( player1 , false ) print ( colorResult ) Return BrickColor ¶ local module = require ( PATH_TO_MODULE ) local part = PATH_TO_PART local players = game : GetService ( "Players" ) local player1 = Players : FindFirstChild ( "Player1" ) local colorResult = module . promptPickColor ( player1 , true , module . Enum . returnType . brickColor ) part . BrickColor = colorResult Return per RGB value ¶ local module = require ( PATH_TO_MODULE ) local part = PATH_TO_PART local players = game : GetService ( "Players" ) local player1 = Players : FindFirstChild ( "Player1" ) local r , g , b = module . promptPickColor ( player1 , true , module . Enum . returnType . perValue ) --// Notice that its returning per RGB value. part . Color = Color3 . fromRGB ( r , g , b ) Return Dictionary ¶ local module = require ( PATH_TO_MODULE ) local part = PATH_TO_PART local players = game : GetService ( "Players" ) local player1 = Players : FindFirstChild ( "Player1" ) local dictionary = module . promptPickColor ( player1 , true , module . Enum . returnType . rgbTableDictionary ) part . Color = Color3 . fromRGB ( dictionary . R , dictionary . G , dictionary . B ) --// Notice that RGB is spelled uppercase.
docs.title: Usage

docs.text: local module = require ( PATH_TO_MODULE ) local players = game : GetService ( "Players" ) local player1 = Players : FindFirstChild ( "Player1" ) local colorResult = module . promptPickColor ( player1 , true ) print ( colorResult )
docs.title: Basic usage using player object

docs.text: local module = require ( PATH_TO_MODULE ) local playerName = "Player1" local colorResult = module . promptPickColor ( playerName , true ) print ( colorResult )
docs.title: Basic usage using player name

docs.text: local module = require ( PATH_TO_MODULE ) local players = game : GetService ( "Players" ) local player1 = Players : FindFirstChild ( "Player1" ) local colorResult = module . promptPickColor ( player1 , false ) print ( colorResult )
docs.title: Non-cancelable prompt

docs.text: local module = require ( PATH_TO_MODULE ) local part = PATH_TO_PART local players = game : GetService ( "Players" ) local player1 = Players : FindFirstChild ( "Player1" ) local colorResult = module . promptPickColor ( player1 , true , module . Enum . returnType . brickColor ) part . BrickColor = colorResult
docs.title: Return BrickColor

docs.text: local module = require ( PATH_TO_MODULE ) local part = PATH_TO_PART local players = game : GetService ( "Players" ) local player1 = Players : FindFirstChild ( "Player1" ) local r , g , b = module . promptPickColor ( player1 , true , module . Enum . returnType . perValue ) --// Notice that its returning per RGB value. part . Color = Color3 . fromRGB ( r , g , b )
docs.title: Return per RGB value

docs.text: local module = require ( PATH_TO_MODULE ) local part = PATH_TO_PART local players = game : GetService ( "Players" ) local player1 = Players : FindFirstChild ( "Player1" ) local dictionary = module . promptPickColor ( player1 , true , module . Enum . returnType . rgbTableDictionary ) part . Color = Color3 . fromRGB ( dictionary . R , dictionary . G , dictionary . B ) --// Notice that RGB is spelled uppercase.
docs.title: Return Dictionary

docs.text: API Reference for Custom Events. If you are looking on how to use, you can go to the usage page . [CustomEventObject] module.newEvent(name, isHidden, parent) ¶ Makes a new custom event. Arguments: name - [Required] [String] The name of the event. isHidden - [Optional] [Bool] If true, module.getEvent() won't work for this event (default is true) parent - [Optional] [Instance] The parent of the event (yes this module uses BindableEvents) Returns CustomEventObject. [CustomEventObject/nil] module.getEvent(eventName) ¶ Gets a custom event from it's name. Warning This will return nil instead if the event has isHidden set to true. Arguments: eventName - [Required] [String] The custom event name. Returns the event/nil if the event doesnt exist or the event has isHidden set to true. [Void] CustomEventObject:Destroy() ¶ Destroys the CustomEventObject. Warning You should NOT call destroy directly on the bindable event. And you should set the variable that you made the CustomEventObject to nil or replace it. Returns nothing. [BindableEvent] CustomEventObject.BindableEvent ¶ Returns the BindableEvent. [Void] CustomEventObject.BindableEvent:Fire(...) ¶ Fires the custom event object, resulting the even connection to initiate. Returns nothing Arguments: ... - [Optional] Arguments that will get passed [RBXScriptSignal] CustomEventObject.Event ¶ Gets the event's RBXScriptSignal. Returns RBXScriptSignal.
docs.title: API Reference

docs.text: Makes a new custom event. Arguments: name - [Required] [String] The name of the event. isHidden - [Optional] [Bool] If true, module.getEvent() won't work for this event (default is true) parent - [Optional] [Instance] The parent of the event (yes this module uses BindableEvents) Returns CustomEventObject.
docs.title: [CustomEventObject] module.newEvent(name, isHidden, parent)

docs.text: Gets a custom event from it's name. Warning This will return nil instead if the event has isHidden set to true. Arguments: eventName - [Required] [String] The custom event name. Returns the event/nil if the event doesnt exist or the event has isHidden set to true.
docs.title: [CustomEventObject/nil] module.getEvent(eventName)

docs.text: Destroys the CustomEventObject. Warning You should NOT call destroy directly on the bindable event. And you should set the variable that you made the CustomEventObject to nil or replace it. Returns nothing.
docs.title: [Void] CustomEventObject:Destroy()

docs.text: Returns the BindableEvent.
docs.title: [BindableEvent] CustomEventObject.BindableEvent

docs.text: Fires the custom event object, resulting the even connection to initiate. Returns nothing Arguments: ... - [Optional] Arguments that will get passed
docs.title: [Void] CustomEventObject.BindableEvent:Fire(...)

docs.text: Gets the event's RBXScriptSignal. Returns RBXScriptSignal.
docs.title: [RBXScriptSignal] CustomEventObject.Event

docs.text: Used to make custom events/call something if something is finished to continue (without using "repeat wait until ...") This stuff is usually simple to make, but I decided to make a module for it. I kinda don't recommend using this but that's up to you. (It's because I have no idea how to make this in the first place, and the code is a bad code.) You can get it here . Pages ¶ API References Usage
docs.title: Custom Events

docs.text: API References Usage
docs.title: Pages

docs.text: This is the example usage of how to use Custom Events. Using it to avoid using wait() ¶ local module = require ( PATH_TO_MODULE ) local eventA = module . newEvent ( "eventA" , true ) --// We create a hidden event named eventA function doSomething () --// Bla bla bla code --// Hey I am finished. eventA . BindableEvent : Fire () end doSomething () eventA . Event : Wait () Making a custom event that can be used in a module script. ¶ local module = require ( PATH_TO_MODULE ) local mapChanged = module . newEvent ( "MapChanged" , false , script ) --// We create a public event named MapChanged and parent it to the script. function changeMap () --// Code --// Done mapChanged . BindableEvent : Fire () end --// Some other script local module = require ( PATH_TO_MODULE ) local mapChangedEvent = module . getEvent ( "MapChanged" ) if mapChangedEvent then mapChangedEvent . Event : Connect ( function () --// What happen if the map changes. end ) end
docs.title: Usage

docs.text: local module = require ( PATH_TO_MODULE ) local eventA = module . newEvent ( "eventA" , true ) --// We create a hidden event named eventA function doSomething () --// Bla bla bla code --// Hey I am finished. eventA . BindableEvent : Fire () end doSomething () eventA . Event : Wait ()
docs.title: Using it to avoid using wait()

docs.text: local module = require ( PATH_TO_MODULE ) local mapChanged = module . newEvent ( "MapChanged" , false , script ) --// We create a public event named MapChanged and parent it to the script. function changeMap () --// Code --// Done mapChanged . BindableEvent : Fire () end --// Some other script local module = require ( PATH_TO_MODULE ) local mapChangedEvent = module . getEvent ( "MapChanged" ) if mapChangedEvent then mapChangedEvent . Event : Connect ( function () --// What happen if the map changes. end ) end
docs.title: Making a custom event that can be used in a module script.

docs.text: This is the API Reference for Device Info module. For usage how to use, see the usage page Expect several grammar problems in this page, my native language is not English. Warning Remember that you can only use this module on the client, not the server. [module.Enum.PlatformType] module.getDevicePlatform() ¶ Returns the client's Platform type. (Example: Mobile, console, and PC) [module.Enum.DeviceType] module.getDeviceType() ¶ Returns the client's device type. (example: Tablet, Phone, PC) [module.Enum.InputType module.getInputType() ¶ Returns the client's input type. (example: Keyboard and mouse, gamepad) [module.Enum.DeviceOrientation/nil] module.getDeviceOrientation() ¶ Returns the current device orientation if the user is on a mobile device, or else nil. Warning Remember you should be using this if the device is a mobile device, or else it will return nil. [module.Enum.GraphicsQuality] module.getGraphicsQuality() ¶ Returns the current graphics level quality. [Vector2] module.getDeviceScreenSize() ¶ Returns the Roblox window size in pixels. [RBXScriptSignal] module.inputTypeChanged ¶ Returns a RBXScriptSignal, fires with module.Enum.InputType when the device input type changes. [RBXScriptSignal] module.screenSizeChanged ¶ Returns a RBXScriptSignal, fires with the new screen size's Vector2 when the Roblox window size changed in pixels. [RBXScriptSignal] module.screenOrientationChanged ¶ Returns a RBXScriptSignal, fires with module.Enum.DeviceOrientation when the mobile device's orientation changed. Will not fire if the device isn't on mobile. [RBXScriptSignal] module.graphicsQualityChanged ¶ Returns a RBXScriptSignal, fires with module.Enum.GraphicsQuality. [PlatformType] module.Enum.PlatformType ¶ This enum is used for module.getDevicePlatform() . Contains: module.Enum.PlatformType.Computer - Indicates that the client's platform type is a computer. module.Enum.PlatformType.Console - Indicates that the client's platform type is a console. module.Enum.PlatformType.Mobile - Indicates that the client's platform type is a mobile device. [DeviceType] module.Enum.DeviceType ¶ This enum is used for module.getDeviceType() . Contains: module.Enum.DeviceType.Computer - Indicates that the client's device type is a computer. module.Enum.DeviceType.Console - Indicates that the client's device type is a console. module.Enum.DeviceType.Phone - Indicates that the client's device type is a phone. module.Enum.DeviceType.Tablet - Indicates that the client's device type is a tablet. module.Enum.DeviceType.TouchscreenComputer - Indicates that the client's device type is a touchscreen computer. [DeviceOrientation] module.Enum.DeviceOrientation ¶ This enum is used for module.getDeviceOrientation() . Contains: module.Enum.DeviceOrientation.Landascape - Indicates that the client's device orientation is landascape. module.Enum.DeviceOrientation.Portrait - Indicates that the client's device orientation is portrait. [InputType] module.Enum.InputType ¶ This enum is used for module.getInputType() . Contains: module.Enum.InputType.Touchscreen - Indicates that the client's input type is touchscreen. module.Enum.InputType.KeyboardMouse - Indicates that the client's input type is keyboard with mouse. module.Enum.InputType.Gamepad - Indicates that the client's input type is gamepad. module.Enum.InputType.VR - Indicates that the client's input type is a VR. module.Enum.InputType.Keyboard - Indicates that the client's input type is a keyboard. module.Enum.InputType.Mouse - Indicates that the client's input type is a mouse. [GraphicsQuality] module.Enum.GraphicsQuality ¶ This enum is used for module.getGraphicsQuality() . Contains: module.Enum.GraphicsQuality.Automatic - Indicates that the client's current graphics quality is automatic. module.Enum.GraphicsQuality.Level01 - Indicates that the client's current graphics quality is level 01. module.Enum.GraphicsQuality.Level02 - Indicates that the client's current graphics quality is level 02. module.Enum.GraphicsQuality.Level03 - Indicates that the client's current graphics quality is level 03. module.Enum.GraphicsQuality.Level04 - Indicates that the client's current graphics quality is level 04. module.Enum.GraphicsQuality.Level05 - Indicates that the client's current graphics quality is level 05. module.Enum.GraphicsQuality.Level06 - Indicates that the client's current graphics quality is level 06. module.Enum.GraphicsQuality.Level07 - Indicates that the client's current graphics quality is level 07. module.Enum.GraphicsQuality.Level08 - Indicates that the client's current graphics quality is level 08. module.Enum.GraphicsQuality.Level09 - Indicates that the client's current graphics quality is level 09. module.Enum.GraphicsQuality.Level10 - Indicates that the client's current graphics quality is level 10.
docs.title: API Reference

docs.text: I actually made this because I got intrested on replicating World Builder's device detection system . After I made the basics, I decided to extend it further by adding graphics level detection, screen resolution detection, device orientation detection for mobile devices, mobile device type detection (phone or tablet). This includes events when something changed (example: device orientation, screen resolution, graphics level) This module can only be used on the client! You can get it here or you can play the module in action at live game here (It's uncopylocked) The detection should be mostly accurate as possible. And I have tested this module countless of times (just telling). Pages ¶ API Reference Usage
docs.title: Device Info

docs.text: API Reference Usage
docs.title: Pages

docs.text: Usage example of how to use the module. For API Reference, see the API Reference page . Log device info and changes to the console ¶ local module = require ( game . ReplicatedStorage . DeviceInfo ) local platform , deviceType , screenResolution , inputType , screenOrientation , graphicsQuality local mPlatform , mDeviceType , mScreenResolution = module . getDevicePlatform (), module . getDeviceType (), module . getDeviceScreenSize () local mInputType = module . getInputType () local mScreenOrientation = module . getDeviceOrientation () local mGraphicsQuality = module . getGraphicsQuality () if mPlatform == module . Enum . PlatformType . Computer then platform = "Computer" elseif mPlatform == module . Enum . PlatformType . Console then platform = "Console" elseif mPlatform == module . Enum . PlatformType . Mobile then platform = "Mobile" end if mDeviceType == module . Enum . DeviceType . Computer then deviceType = "Computer" elseif mDeviceType == module . Enum . DeviceType . Console then deviceType = "Console" elseif mDeviceType == module . Enum . DeviceType . Phone then deviceType = "Phone" elseif mDeviceType == module . Enum . DeviceType . Tablet then deviceType = "Tablet" elseif mDeviceType == module . Enum . DeviceType . TouchscreenComputer then deviceType = "Touchscreen computer" end if mInputType == module . Enum . InputType . Gamepad then inputType = "Gamepad" elseif mInputType == module . Enum . InputType . Keyboard then inputType = "Keyboard" elseif mInputType == module . Enum . InputType . Mouse then inputType = "Mouse" elseif mInputType == module . Enum . InputType . KeyboardMouse then inputType = "Keyboard and mouse" elseif mInputType == module . Enum . InputType . Touchscreen then inputType = "Touchscreen" elseif mInputType == module . Enum . InputType . VR then inputType = "VR" end if mPlatform == module . Enum . PlatformType . Mobile then local mScreenOrientation = module . getDeviceOrientation () if mScreenOrientation == module . Enum . DeviceOrientation . Landscape then screenOrientation = "Lanadscape" elseif mScreenOrientation == module . Enum . DeviceOrientation . Portrait then screenOrientation = "Portrait" end else screenOrientation = "Only supported on Mobile devices" end if mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Automatic then graphicsQuality = "Automatic" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level01 then graphicsQuality = "Level 01" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level02 then graphicsQuality = "Level 02" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level03 then graphicsQuality = "Level 03" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level04 then graphicsQuality = "Level 04" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level05 then graphicsQuality = "Level 05" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level06 then graphicsQuality = "Level 06" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level07 then graphicsQuality = "Level 07" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level08 then graphicsQuality = "Level 08" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level09 then graphicsQuality = "Level 09" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level10 then graphicsQuality = "Level 10" end print ( "----------------------------------" ) print ( " Platform: " .. platform ) print ( " Device type: " .. deviceType ) print ( " Screen resolution: " .. mScreenResolution . X .. " x " .. mScreenResolution . Y ) print ( " Input type: " .. inputType ) print ( " Graphics quality level: " .. graphicsQuality ) print ( " Screen orientation: " .. screenOrientation ) print ( "----------------------------------" ) module . inputTypeChanged : Connect ( function ( newInputType ) if newInputType == module . Enum . InputType . Gamepad then print ( "Input type changed to Gamepad" ) elseif newInputType == module . Enum . InputType . Keyboard then print ( "Input type changed to Keyboard" ) elseif newInputType == module . Enum . InputType . Mouse then print ( "Input type changed to Mouse" ) elseif newInputType == module . Enum . InputType . KeyboardMouse then print ( "Input type changed to Keyboard and mouse" ) elseif newInputType == module . Enum . InputType . Touchscreen then print ( "Input type changed to Touchscreen" ) elseif newInputType == module . Enum . InputType . VR then print ( "Input type changed to VR" ) end end ) print ( "Listening for input type changes." ) module . screenSizeChanged : Connect ( function ( newSize ) print ( "Resolution changed: " .. newSize . X .. " x " .. newSize . Y ) end ) print ( "Listening for resolution changes." ) module . screenOrientationChanged : Connect ( function ( newOrientation ) if newOrientation == module . Enum . DeviceOrientation . Portrait then print ( "Screen orientation changed to Portrait" ) elseif newOrientation == module . Enum . DeviceOrientation . Landscape then print ( "Screen orientation changed to Landscape" ) end end ) print ( "Listening for orientation changes." ) module . graphicsQualityChanged : Connect ( function ( newQuality ) if newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Automatic then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Automatic" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level01 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 01" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level02 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 02" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level03 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 03" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level04 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 04" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level05 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 05" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level06 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 06" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level07 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 07" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level08 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 08" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level09 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 09" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level10 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 10" ) end end ) print ( "Listening for graphic quality changes." ) The only example is above becase I am lazy at the moment I am writing and I have limited time.
docs.title: Usage

docs.text: local module = require ( game . ReplicatedStorage . DeviceInfo ) local platform , deviceType , screenResolution , inputType , screenOrientation , graphicsQuality local mPlatform , mDeviceType , mScreenResolution = module . getDevicePlatform (), module . getDeviceType (), module . getDeviceScreenSize () local mInputType = module . getInputType () local mScreenOrientation = module . getDeviceOrientation () local mGraphicsQuality = module . getGraphicsQuality () if mPlatform == module . Enum . PlatformType . Computer then platform = "Computer" elseif mPlatform == module . Enum . PlatformType . Console then platform = "Console" elseif mPlatform == module . Enum . PlatformType . Mobile then platform = "Mobile" end if mDeviceType == module . Enum . DeviceType . Computer then deviceType = "Computer" elseif mDeviceType == module . Enum . DeviceType . Console then deviceType = "Console" elseif mDeviceType == module . Enum . DeviceType . Phone then deviceType = "Phone" elseif mDeviceType == module . Enum . DeviceType . Tablet then deviceType = "Tablet" elseif mDeviceType == module . Enum . DeviceType . TouchscreenComputer then deviceType = "Touchscreen computer" end if mInputType == module . Enum . InputType . Gamepad then inputType = "Gamepad" elseif mInputType == module . Enum . InputType . Keyboard then inputType = "Keyboard" elseif mInputType == module . Enum . InputType . Mouse then inputType = "Mouse" elseif mInputType == module . Enum . InputType . KeyboardMouse then inputType = "Keyboard and mouse" elseif mInputType == module . Enum . InputType . Touchscreen then inputType = "Touchscreen" elseif mInputType == module . Enum . InputType . VR then inputType = "VR" end if mPlatform == module . Enum . PlatformType . Mobile then local mScreenOrientation = module . getDeviceOrientation () if mScreenOrientation == module . Enum . DeviceOrientation . Landscape then screenOrientation = "Lanadscape" elseif mScreenOrientation == module . Enum . DeviceOrientation . Portrait then screenOrientation = "Portrait" end else screenOrientation = "Only supported on Mobile devices" end if mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Automatic then graphicsQuality = "Automatic" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level01 then graphicsQuality = "Level 01" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level02 then graphicsQuality = "Level 02" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level03 then graphicsQuality = "Level 03" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level04 then graphicsQuality = "Level 04" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level05 then graphicsQuality = "Level 05" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level06 then graphicsQuality = "Level 06" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level07 then graphicsQuality = "Level 07" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level08 then graphicsQuality = "Level 08" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level09 then graphicsQuality = "Level 09" elseif mGraphicsQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level10 then graphicsQuality = "Level 10" end print ( "----------------------------------" ) print ( " Platform: " .. platform ) print ( " Device type: " .. deviceType ) print ( " Screen resolution: " .. mScreenResolution . X .. " x " .. mScreenResolution . Y ) print ( " Input type: " .. inputType ) print ( " Graphics quality level: " .. graphicsQuality ) print ( " Screen orientation: " .. screenOrientation ) print ( "----------------------------------" ) module . inputTypeChanged : Connect ( function ( newInputType ) if newInputType == module . Enum . InputType . Gamepad then print ( "Input type changed to Gamepad" ) elseif newInputType == module . Enum . InputType . Keyboard then print ( "Input type changed to Keyboard" ) elseif newInputType == module . Enum . InputType . Mouse then print ( "Input type changed to Mouse" ) elseif newInputType == module . Enum . InputType . KeyboardMouse then print ( "Input type changed to Keyboard and mouse" ) elseif newInputType == module . Enum . InputType . Touchscreen then print ( "Input type changed to Touchscreen" ) elseif newInputType == module . Enum . InputType . VR then print ( "Input type changed to VR" ) end end ) print ( "Listening for input type changes." ) module . screenSizeChanged : Connect ( function ( newSize ) print ( "Resolution changed: " .. newSize . X .. " x " .. newSize . Y ) end ) print ( "Listening for resolution changes." ) module . screenOrientationChanged : Connect ( function ( newOrientation ) if newOrientation == module . Enum . DeviceOrientation . Portrait then print ( "Screen orientation changed to Portrait" ) elseif newOrientation == module . Enum . DeviceOrientation . Landscape then print ( "Screen orientation changed to Landscape" ) end end ) print ( "Listening for orientation changes." ) module . graphicsQualityChanged : Connect ( function ( newQuality ) if newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Automatic then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Automatic" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level01 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 01" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level02 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 02" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level03 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 03" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level04 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 04" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level05 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 05" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level06 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 06" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level07 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 07" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level08 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 08" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level09 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 09" ) elseif newQuality == module . Enum . GraphicsQuality . Level10 then print ( "Graphic quality level changed to Level 10" ) end end ) print ( "Listening for graphic quality changes." ) The only example is above becase I am lazy at the moment I am writing and I have limited time.
